title: Archive: Agent Society / Game World Model Background
description: Archive reason: multi-agent society and game-world modeling are useful background, but active work is already represented by OASIS, Self-Improving Agents, and UMI world-model cards.
summary: The game-society / multi-agent world-model idea is archived as background. It is too broad and overlaps existing active cards unless it becomes a robot-task simulator or evaluation generator.

details.text: OASIS covers scalable LLM agent society simulation, while Gamma-World and MultiWorld cover recent multi-agent or multi-view generative world-model directions. These are references, not a separate dashboard project today.

details: Moved-out live work: Self-Improving Agents owns agentic simulation/asset generation; UMI owns robot manipulation world modeling.
Archive decision: keep only as literature/background until it proposes a first robot-task simulator experiment.
risks_decisions: Reason type: background/overlap with existing active cards.
Reopen condition: a game/society world model becomes a concrete source of robot tasks, reward functions, or training data.
